Then, the remainder of $(5^{3})^{670}$ when it is divided by 7 is the same as the remainder of $6^{670}$ when it is divided by 7.  Over here, use Euler's theorem.  Notice that $\phi(7) = 6$.  Then the remainder when 670 is divided by 6 is 4.  Thus, $6^{670}$ has the same remainder when divided by 7 as $6^{4}$ does.  $6^{2} = 36$ has a remainder of 1 when divided by 7 and notice we have $6^{4} = (6^{2})^{2}$.
